source_pdf_name: NovelSeek - When Agent Becomes the Scientist.pdf
coverage_note: Extracted from PDF physical pages 2-9 (Abstract; Introduction; Sec. 2 InternAgent module descriptions covering Survey Agent, Code Review Agent, Idea Innovation Agent, Assessment Agent, Human-interactive Feedback, Orchestration Agent, Sec. 2.2 Methodology Development Agent, Sec. 2.3 Exception-Guided Debugging Framework and Experimental Planning and Adaptive Evolution), plus selected results/discussion pages 13 (Table 3 experiment statistics), 20 (Fig. 7 caption; Human Evaluation intro), and 22 (Conclusion and Future Works). Per-task dataset/baseline descriptions (pg 10-12), Table 1/2 result cells (pg 13-14), Related Works (pg 21), and appendices (pg 23-34) are not covered. The paper's PDF title page identifies the system as 'InternAgent' (rebrand of the citation-key system 'NovelSeek'); this ledger quotes the PDF text as extracted, which uses 'InternAgent' throughout the body. No judgment, alignment, or closure status is stored here (charter B.1). Frozen boundary per task brief: version 2025-internagent, configuration reported-multi-agent, task regime twelve-computational-tasks.

propositions.exact_quote: InternAgent provides an interface for human expert feedback and multi-agent interaction in automated end-to-end processes, allowing for the seamless integration of domain expert knowledge.

we propose the InternAgent, an end-to-end auto-research pipeline, which covers four main modules: self-evolving idea generation, human-interactive feedback, idea-to-methodology construction, and multi-round experiment planning and execution.
propositions.notes_verbatim: names four main modules: self-evolving idea generation, human-interactive feedback, idea-to-methodology construction, multi-round experiment planning and execution

propositions.exact_quote: InternAgent, a unified closed-loop scientific research framework that can automate the entire research cycle, including idea generation, idea-to-methodology transformation, experiment execution, and result feedback.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'can automate' -- capability framing

propositions.exact_quote: InternAgent offers interactive interfaces for human-machine collaboration within the idea generation module and across the entire system.

By selecting collaboration modes, such as leveraging AI or human experts, it provides evaluations of idea generation effectiveness and facilitates the assessment, reflection, and documentation of experimental results.
named_outputs: assessment, reflection, and documentation of experimental results
propositions.notes_verbatim: subject 'it' (InternAgent) referenced by pronoun only in this sentence; not counted as named

propositions.exact_quote: We conducted extensive human studies centered around InternAgent, including inviting domain experts to evaluate and score the novelty of ideas generated by InternAgent, and comparing the research efficiency between human researchers and InternAgent.

The primary responsibility of the Survey Agent is to search for scientific papers, offering two distinct modes to address the varying needs for depth and breadth in literature research during the scientific discovery process: 1) the literature review mode and 2) the deep research mode.
propositions.notes_verbatim: names two modes: literature review mode, deep research mode

propositions.exact_quote: In the literature review mode, the Survey Agent deconstructs the research task into multiple keyword combinations, enabling a broad search across various academic databases.
In the deep research mode, following the initial literature survey, the Survey Agent downloads and thoroughly examines the full texts of relevant scientific papers.
named_inputs: full texts of relevant scientific papers

locator.section: 2.1 Self-Evolving Idea Generation -- Code Review Agent

propositions.exact_quote: The Code Review Agent is crucial for understanding baseline codes for different research tasks, serving as a foundation for innovation by identifying improvements and developing new methodologies.

locator.section: 2.1 Self-Evolving Idea Generation -- Idea Innovation Agent

propositions.exact_quote: In the context of idea generation, the agent utilizes a general LLM configured with a higher temperature setting. This configuration encourages the generation of more diverse and creative outputs.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'the agent' = Idea Innovation Agent per section heading, but not named by proper name within this exact quote; high-temperature LLM ideation mechanism

locator.section: 2.1 Self-Evolving Idea Generation -- Idea Innovation Agent

propositions.exact_quote: This enables the agent to identify patterns and insights that might be overlooked in traditional research, generating novel hypotheses and strategies based on task definitions, baseline methods, and current scientific knowledge.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'enables' -- capability framing; idea generation explicitly task-defined via task definitions input

locator.section: 2.1 Self-Evolving Idea Generation -- Idea Innovation Agent

propositions.exact_quote: Idea evolution leverages the capabilities of an LLM to improve existing ideas. The process involves analyzing the content of these ideas, incorporating reflections, which include evaluations of novelty, feasibility, and scientific validity, and integrating insights from related literature.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'reflections' explicitly glossed as evaluations of novelty, feasibility, and scientific validity

propositions.exact_quote: The primary responsibility of the Assessment Agent is to critically evaluate ideas using multidimensional scoring. Each idea is analyzed across key dimensions: coherence, credibility, verifiability, novelty, and alignment.
propositions.notes_verbatim: five named scoring dimensions: coherence, credibility, verifiability, novelty, alignment

propositions.exact_quote: It assigns scores from 0 to 10, which are combined using a weighted summation to produce an overall score for each idea, aiding in the ranking process.
This capability prevents high-scoring ideas from being overly similar or derived from the same original concept.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'This capability' -- capability framing, diversity constraint among top-ranked ideas

propositions.exact_quote: The human-interactive feedback mechanism of InternAgent is categorized into two primary types: 1) feedback directly provided by humans and 2) feedback automatically generated by agent.
propositions.notes_verbatim: two feedback types named: human-provided, agent-generated

propositions.exact_quote: Human-provided feedback can address one or multiple ideas, offering insights and critiques that lead to further refinement and adjustment of these ideas based on the feedback received.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'can address' -- capability framing

propositions.exact_quote: It also oversees the timing of human feedback, especially for high-scoring ideas.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'It' = Orchestration Agent, named earlier in the same paragraph but not within this exact quote

propositions.exact_quote: Additionally, it determines optimal points for human feedback, integrating expert insights after identifying high-scoring ideas to refine and adapt them, aligning outputs with user requirements.
propositions.notes_verbatim: human-authority touchpoint: expert insights integrated after high-scoring ideas identified, prior to further refinement

propositions.exact_quote: Figure 3: InternAgent Self-evolutionary path of ideas for reaction yield prediction task.
propositions.notes_verbatim: figure caption; figure itself depicts a branching lineage (Init Idea 0/1/2 -> Evolved Idea 0-0/0-1/0-2 -> Evolved Idea 0-0-0/0-0-1/0-0-2 -> Evolved Idea 0-0-1-0/0-0-1-1/0-0-1-2) of successively revised idea text -- diagram content not separately quoted here

propositions.exact_quote: Specifically, to develop a comprehensive method corresponding to the concise research idea, the Method Development Agent possesses two core capabilities: 1) Methodology Initialization: which involves constructing the basic structure and content of a method by integrating the idea with baseline codes and the methodology content of relevant literature; 2) Methodology Refinement: which iteratively enhances the basic method structure for the purpose of rigor and completeness, ensuring a more detailed and robust methodology.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'possesses two core capabilities' -- capability framing; two capabilities named: Methodology Initialization, Methodology Refinement

propositions.exact_quote: The agent conducts a comprehensive analysis of the initial methodology M, incorporating structured critiques C , which include both automated assessments and expert human feedback.
propositions.notes_verbatim: critique C explicitly glossed as including automated assessments and expert human feedback

propositions.exact_quote: This framework operates by systematically capturing runtime exceptions during execution attempts, analyzing error contexts, and formulating targeted fixes through reasoning of the large language model.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'this framework' = exception-guided debugging framework named on the preceding page

propositions.exact_quote: Our coder module employs a dual-strategy approach according to the complexity of given baseline code. For single-file or limited-scope implementation tasks, we use the Aider coding assistant (Gauthier & Contributors, 2023), which facilitates localized code modifications with minimal overhead.

For complex repository-level codes requiring comprehensive structural understanding across different functions, we deploy OpenHands framework (Wang et al., 2024b), which enables thorough codebase analysis and coordinated multi-file modifications while maintaining the integrity of the overall code architecture.

The debugging process follows a systematic cycle: (1) execution attempt, (2) exception capture and traceback analysis, (3) contextual code structure understanding, (4) debugging strategy formulation, and (5) targeted implementation. This cycle continues iteratively until successful execution or reaching a predefined iteration threshold.
propositions.notes_verbatim: five-step debugging cycle named; termination condition = successful execution OR predefined iteration threshold

propositions.exact_quote: Rather than employing a single-pass implementation strategy, we designed an adaptive evolution approach for our implementation process. This approach involves structured iterations where each implementation attempt is followed by performance assessment and potential refinement.
propositions.notes_verbatim: first clause ('we designed') is a past-tense authorial design decision; remainder describes the iteration structure procedurally

propositions.exact_quote: We maintain records of implementation decisions across iterations, which helps track changes and their corresponding effects. This directed adaptation process enables the gradual refinement of complex implementations based on empirical results rather than theoretical assumptions alone.
propositions.notes_verbatim: implementation decisions across iterations explicitly recorded/tracked; refinement basis contrasted as empirical results vs. theoretical assumptions

propositions.exact_quote: Table 3: Experiments statistics across different tasks. Each cell shows the number of ideas that improved performance, the number of ideas that successfully ran, and the total number of ideas tested (format: improved / successful / tested).
named_outputs: number of ideas that improved performance
number of ideas that successfully ran

propositions.exact_quote: Figure 7: Visual Examples of Experimental Planning and Adaptive Evolution on Auto3DCls task.
propositions.notes_verbatim: figure depicts alternating Initial/Evolutionary Planning steps and Performance Reflection outcomes (e.g. '+0.5% Acc improvement', '-0.8% Acc degradation') that precede the next planning step -- diagram labels not separately quoted here

propositions.exact_quote: Each task involves generating 20 ideas, which are evaluated by five qualified reviewers. The assessments focus on four criteria: soundness, contribution, overall rating, and confidence.
propositions.notes_verbatim: four named evaluation criteria: soundness, contribution, overall rating, confidence

propositions.exact_quote: InternAgent refines the initially generated ideas through human-interactive feedback enriched with a self-evolutionary path of ideas. It facilitates the transformation from coarse-grained proposals to fine-grained methodologies via an idea-to-methodology construction process.

Furthermore, by leveraging multi-round experimental planning and execution, it implements the corresponding theoretical methods, thereby completing the closed-loop process in scientific research—from hypothesis generation to verification.
